title: ZeRO: Zero Redundancy Optimizer
concept: ZeRO eliminates memory redundancy by partitioning optimizer states, gradients, and model parameters across GPUs during training, saving massive memory.

exercises.prompt: What is the main reason developers get an 'Out of Memory' error when training modern giant AI models?

options.text: The model is too large to fit in the GPU's memory.
The GPU runs out of electric power.
The code is written in Python instead of C++.

exercises.explain: Memory limit is the primary bottleneck when training giant AI models. If the model parameters and training states don't fit in the GPU memory, training cannot start.

cards.title: Running Out of GPU Memory?
cards.body: Have you ever tried to run a giant AI model only to get the dreaded "Out of Memory" error? As models grow, they quickly overflow the memory of even the most powerful GPUs. ZeRO is the ultimate memory-saving hack that lets us train giant models without buying bigger hardware.

cards.title: What is ZeRO?
cards.body: ZeRO (Zero Redundancy Optimizer) is a smart memory management technology. Instead of keeping a full copy of the AI's training data (like weights and optimizer settings) on every single GPU, ZeRO slices this data up and distributes the pieces across all available GPUs, putting it back together only when needed.

cards.title: The Split Textbook Project
cards.body: Imagine a group project where four students need to edit a giant textbook. Instead of buying four identical, expensive copies of the whole book (one for each student), they tear the book into four sections. Each student is responsible for holding and updating their own section. When someone needs a page they don't have, they quickly ask a teammate for a copy, use it, and then throw it away to save desk space.

cards.title: ZeRO in Action
cards.body: When training a model using ZeRO-1, the optimizer states (which track how the model learns and consume the most memory) are split. If you have 4 GPUs, each GPU only stores 1/4 of the optimizer states. This simple split can reduce the memory footprint by up to 4 times without slowing down training!

cards.title: Does it Slow Us Down?
cards.body: People think that to save memory, ZeRO must make AI training run much slower. That is not true! By using clever, high-speed communication between GPUs, ZeRO achieves massive memory savings with almost zero impact on training speed.

cards.title: Classic vs. ZeRO Memory
cards.body: See how memory is distributed across two GPUs before and after using ZeRO.

left.text: Both GPU 1 and GPU 2 hold identical copies of all parameters and optimizer states, wasting half the total memory.

right.text: GPU 1 holds part A, and GPU 2 holds part B. They share their parts instantly when needed, doubling free memory!

exercises.prompt: Why does standard GPU training waste so much memory before we use ZeRO?

options.text: Every GPU keeps an identical copy of all parameters and optimizer states.
GPUs share memory automatically without any network cables.
Data is processed one single word at a time.
The model is split into layers and run sequentially.

exercises.explain: Standard data parallel training duplicates all model parameters, gradients, and optimizer states on every single GPU, leading to massive memory waste (redundancy).

exercises.prompt: True or False: ZeRO stands for Zero Redundancy Optimizer.
exercises.explain: ZeRO stands for Zero Redundancy Optimizer. Its primary goal is to eliminate redundant memory storage across GPUs.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete how ZeRO saves memory.
exercises.sentence: ZeRO eliminates redundancy by [blank] the optimizer states and parameters across GPUs.
exercises.explain: ZeRO works by partitioning (slicing up) the memory-heavy components across all available GPUs, rather than duplicating them on every GPU.

exercises.prompt: Identify the AI-generated lie about ZeRO:

options.text: ZeRO duplicates all parameters on every GPU to ensure maximum backup safety.
ZeRO stands for Zero Redundancy Optimizer.
ZeRO partitions optimizer states, gradients, and parameters.

exercises.explain: The first statement is AI-generated and false because ZeRO actively splits data to save memory, rather than duplicating it. The others correctly describe ZeRO's features.

exercises.prompt: You are setting up training for a massive language model that is too big to fit on a single GPU's memory. Which level of ZeRO should you use to get the absolute maximum GPU memory savings by partitioning all three main memory components?

options.text: ZeRO-1, because it only splits the optimizer states to keep things simple.
ZeRO-2, because it keeps parameters copied on every single GPU.
ZeRO-3, because it partitions optimizer states, gradients, and parameters.
ZeRO-Offload, because it avoids using GPU memory entirely.

exercises.explain: ZeRO-3 splits all three main memory hogs (optimizer states, gradients, and model parameters) across all your GPUs. This provides the maximum memory savings possible, allowing you to train massive models that wouldn't otherwise fit.
